title: Umpires, Referees, and Other Sports Officials
description: Officiate at competitive athletic or sporting events. Detect infractions of rules and decide penalties according to established regulations. Includes all sporting officials, referees, and competition judges.

tasks.text: Officiate at sporting events, games, or competitions, to maintain standards of play and to ensure that game rules are observed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect game sites for compliance with regulations or safety requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Resolve claims of rule infractions or complaints by participants and assess any necessary penalties, according to regulations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Signal participants or other officials to make them aware of infractions or to otherwise regulate play or competition.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Teach and explain the rules and regulations governing a specific sport.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inspect sporting equipment or examine participants to ensure compliance with event and safety regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Report to regulating organizations regarding sporting activities, complaints made, and actions taken or needed, such as fines or other disciplinary actions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with other sporting officials, coaches, players, and facility managers to provide information, coordinate activities, and discuss problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Judge performances in sporting competitions to award points, impose scoring penalties, and determine results.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Verify scoring calculations before competition winners are announced.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compile scores and other athletic records.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Verify credentials of participants in sporting events, and make other qualifying determinations, such as starting order or handicap number.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Keep track of event times, including race times and elapsed time during game segments, starting or stopping play when necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct participants to assigned areas, such as starting blocks or penalty areas.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Research and study players and teams to anticipate issues that might arise in future engagements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
